waterTable.evidenceSnippet: Groundwater was encountered at 12.5 feet below existing grade during drilling of Boring B-1.
Free water was noted at 14 feet in Boring B-2, consistent with regional water table data.
Groundwater seepage observed at 11 feet during auger advancement in Boring B-3.
No groundwater was encountered to the maximum depth explored (25 ft) in Boring B-4.
Water table encountered at approximately 13.5 feet below ground surface in Boring B-5.
Groundwater was observed at 15 feet during drilling operations at Boring B-6.

borings.notes: Upper 5 feet consists of stiff lean clay with moderate plasticity. Transition to sandy clay below 10 feet.
Approximately 3 feet of uncontrolled fill encountered at surface. Native lean clay below fill layer.
Fat clay with high liquid limit (LL=62) present from 2 to 8 feet. Significant swell potential noted.
Generally favorable soil conditions. Dense sand layer encountered below 15 feet.
Soft clay zone from 6-10 feet with SPT N-values of 3-5 blows/foot. May require over-excavation.
Consistent soil profile. Moderate bearing capacity expected for shallow foundations.

slab.summary: Slab-on-grade should be constructed on a minimum 12-inch moisture-conditioned pad of select fill material.
Scarification and recompaction of subgrade soils to 95% Standard Proctor density recommended.

foundation.summary: Drilled pier foundations recommended with minimum embedment of 15 feet into native soil below the fill zone.
Shallow spread footings may be used if bearing on competent clay below 5 feet with minimum bearing pressure of 2,500 psf.

pavement.summary: Minimum 6-inch lime-stabilized subgrade recommended for all pavement areas due to expansive clay subgrade.
Undercut of soft subgrade soils to a depth of 12 inches below pavement section where SPT N-values are less than 5.

riskFlags.evidence: Soft clay zones with SPT N-values of 3-5 blows/foot were encountered in Borings B-3 and B-5 between 6-10 feet depth.
riskFlags.description: Areas near B-3 and B-5 may require over-excavation and replacement with engineered fill.

riskFlags.evidence: High plasticity index (PI=35) clays detected in the upper soil profile across the site.
riskFlags.description: Expansive clays require strict moisture conditioning during construction. Volume changes expected with moisture fluctuations.

riskFlags.evidence: Fat clay (CH) with liquid limit of 62 encountered in Boring B-3 from 2 to 8 feet.
riskFlags.description: Fatty soils present localized risk for foundation movement and slab distress.

riskFlags.evidence: Pavement subgrade in some areas shows inadequate bearing capacity with CBR values below 3%.
riskFlags.description: Undercut and replacement with select fill recommended for pavement subgrade improvement.

riskFlags.evidence: Groundwater at 11-15 feet may impact deep excavations for utilities or pier installation.
riskFlags.description: Temporary dewatering may be needed for excavations below 10 feet in some areas.

chatResponses.response: Groundwater was encountered in 5 of 6 borings at depths ranging from 11 to 15 feet. Boring B-4 did not encounter water to the maximum depth explored.
The report recommends drilled pier foundations with minimum 15-foot embedment, or shallow spread footings on competent clay with 2,500 psf bearing pressure.
Key risk flags include: over-excavation needs near B-3 and B-5, moisture control for expansive clays, fatty soils in B-3, and potential dewatering for deep excavations.
